subject: Coming out of retirement

body: Peter, I will be the first to tell you that I have lived a blessed life. I played a game I love. Met the girl of my dreams. Raised a family of five children and 22 grandchildren. I even won a couple of championships along the way. But I still have goals in my life. One is to ensure future generations will have the same opportunities I enjoyed. Never has that been a more important mission of mine than now. That's why I'm emailing Republicans, urging them to get involved in this election today. http://email.granitelists.com/ct/37744494:W1o2g7bNx:m:3:1155937413:8EA63B0C2ABDE4DAD118230F33F084BE:r Speaker Ryan and House Republicans are standing up for Americans. Their plans to lower taxes and reduce the debt will pave the way for more jobs and a stronger economy. But House Republicans face a huge obstacle -- their success is completely dependent upon whether they stay in the majority after the election. They're going into the final quarterly fundraising deadline before the election at a $12.4 million disadvantage. I know how much pressure they're under. When I was down a few strokes, I felt the entire weight of the world on my shoulders. But those are the moments I prepared for and embraced. In those occasions, I often delivered some of the best performances of my career. That's what every single conservative out there has to do right now. We must rise to the challenge before next Friday to help Republicans defeat Nancy Pelosi and Hillary Clinton. That's why I hope you will join me and pitch in to help House Republicans and Speaker Ryan. name: Jason Alexander for RussFeingold.com
subject: It's not you, it's me
disclaimer_text: paid for by Russ for Wisconsin, Joe Sensenbrenner, Madison, WI, Treasurer
body:
On Seinfeld, my character George Costanza made the claim that he created the phrase, it's not you, it's me. | [](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf4576/1c469400/89d89b/357ad84/3878973401/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/rdf-sept?utm_source=ema_2016-09-24_eoq2_nd&refcode=ema_2016-09-24_eoq2_nd&utm_campaign=eoq2&utm_medium=ema") Friend, On "Seinfeld," my character George Costanza made the claim that he created the well-worn phrase, "it's not you, it's me." That phrase was intended to be a creative lie to allow George to get out of a bad situation without hurting anyone's feelings. Thankfully, in real life, it is not a phrase I have needed to employ terribly often. In fact, when it comes to politics and the business of electing our leaders, that phrase really should read, "It is You, and it is Me." Elections are about all of us -- individuals, families, neighborhoods, towns, states, and country. It is about leadership that is progressive, inclusive, and produces real results for the greatest number of people. And because elections are about us -- the "me" is incredibly important. The "me" is the candidate we elect and trust to work on our behalf and address our concerns with the highest integrity and ability. The people of Wisconsin have a powerful choice to make in the upcoming election about who will serve them as senator. My friend Russ Feingold is running a true grassroots campaign that relies on __you__ \-- not on the three super PACs, _ten_ right-wing groups, and the NRA supporting his opponent, Senator Ron Johnson. **And right now, Russ needs our help: He needs to reach his goal of raising $250,000 by midnight on September 30 if he's going to keep up the pace.** **[Please stand with me today, and with Russ Feingold. Contribute $5 right now to help him reach his goal.](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf4576/1c469400/89d89b/357ad84/3878973401/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/rdf-sept?utm_source=ema_2016-09-24_eoq2_nd&refcode=ema_2016-09-24_eoq2_nd&utm_campaign=eoq2&utm_medium=ema")** [](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf4576/1c469400/89d89b/357ad84/3878973401/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/rdf-sept?utm_source=ema_2016-09-24_eoq2_nd&refcode=ema_2016-09-24_eoq2_nd&utm_campaign=eoq2&utm_medium=ema") [](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf4576/1c469400/89d89b/357ad84/3878973401/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/rdf-sept?utm_source=ema_2016-09-24_eoq2_nd&refcode=ema_2016-09-24_eoq2_nd&utm_campaign=eoq2&utm_medium=ema") _Or, if your payment information is saved with ActBlue, click below and your contribution will go through immediately:_ | | [$5 »](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf4576/1c469400/89d89b/357ad85/3878973401/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/rdf-sept?utm_source=ema_2016-09-24_eoq2_nd&refcode=ema_2016-09-24_eoq2_nd&utm_campaign=eoq2&utm_medium=ema&express_lane=true&amount=5")

clean_body: On Seinfeld, my character George Costanza made the claim that he created the phrase, it's not you, it's me. | "" Friend, On "Seinfeld," my character George Costanza made the claim that he created the well-worn phrase, "it's not you, it's me." That phrase was intended to be a creative lie to allow George to get out of a bad situation without hurting anyone's feelings. Thankfully, in real life, it is not a phrase I have needed to employ terribly often. In fact, when it comes to politics and the business of electing our leaders, that phrase really should read, "It is You, and it is Me." Elections are about all of us -- individuals, families, neighborhoods, towns, states, and country. It is about leadership that is progressive, inclusive, and produces real results for the greatest number of people. And because elections are about us -- the "me" is incredibly important. The "me" is the candidate we elect and trust to work on our behalf and address our concerns with the highest integrity and ability. The people of Wisconsin have a powerful choice to make in the upcoming election about who will serve them as senator. My friend Russ Feingold is running a true grassroots campaign that relies on you \-- not on the three super PACs, ten right-wing groups, and the NRA supporting his opponent, Senator Ron Johnson. And right now, Russ needs our help: He needs to reach his goal of raising $250,000 by midnight on September 30 if he's going to keep up the pace. "" "" "" Or, if your payment information is saved with ActBlue, click below and your contribution will go through immediately: | | "" | | "" | "" | | "" Russ has a proven record of serving Wisconsin and a powerful platform from which to serve them again. He is asking for your support, and I am proud to stand beside him in Milwaukee and Madison today to talk to supporters about why he is not only vital for Wisconsin, but why he is vital for the United States Senate and the United States of America. Thanks for doing what you can to elect Russ. Jason Alexander ""

I don’t have lobbyists bankrolling our campaign like Hillary does. But I do have the resilience, courage, and pride of the American people. And there’s NOTHING in the world I’d rather have. Our campaign has raised hundreds of millions of dollars -- all from Americans contributing whatever they can to take back our country. September 25, 2016

**Contact:**
Ethan Latour
[ethan@philscott.org](mailto:ethan@philscott.org)
802-595-3776
_**Scott Celebrates 70th Anniversary of Construction Company, Reveals Plan to Sell his Share if Elected**_
Middlesex, Vt. – At a party Saturday celebrating the 70th anniversary of Dubois Construction, Phil Scott announced to a group of employees, former employees, their families, and supporters that he will sell his share in the company if he is elected the next Governor of Vermont.

In his remarks, Scott shared his personal story and history with Dubois Construction, the “ups and downs,” highlighting some of the challenges of owning a business and how a small, family-run business must tackle adversity in order to survive.

“Running a small business isn’t easy. It takes intestinal fortitude, hard work and a great team,” Scott said. “We had our share of sleepless nights wondering how to make payroll, where our next job was coming from and how do we pay our bills. No business can survive with just buildings and equipment. The core of its success is the people: The employees with the talent to put forth ideas and concepts and make them into reality.”

Recalling what initially sparked his interest in politics, Scott said it was about bringing the voice of small family businesses and their employees to Montpelier.

“Though I would have never guessed it 16 years ago, I have come to understand that this work -- the work of making sure the voice of every working Vermonter and every family is heard in Montpelier -- is never done,” Scott said. “I know this decision is the right one and will assure Vermonters that there is nothing more important to me than serving them and following through on the commitments I am making in this campaign to make the economy and affordability Montpelier’s top priorities, and to restore the faith and trust that has been lost in state government in the last six years.”
###

Friend -- I know our team emails a lot. But today, I wanted to remind you what's at stake in this election. If Indiana elects Congressman Todd Young, we'll have a leader who thinks Social Security is a "fatally flawed" "Ponzi scheme." Who cares more about pleasing the Koch brothers than protecting Hoosier jobs. Who is, any way you slice it, much too extreme for Indiana.

Hoosiers can't afford to have someone like that representing us in the Senate. But without your help, that's who we might end up with. **[Will you chip in $5 now to stop Congressman Young and elect Evan Bayh?](https://bayh.bsd.net/page/m/32a504ca/7be36b49/1e987866/78f87e77/3197926088/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/bayh-email?refcode=20160925nd&amount=10")**

Evan represents a path for Indiana and our country that is the direct opposite of Congressman Young's. He's repeatedly stood up for Indiana's working families by voting to protect Social Security and Medicare and eliminate discrimination by health insurance companies. As governor, he worked across the aisle to balance our state's budget and cut taxes.

**That 's the kind of Hoosier common sense we need in Washington!**

Most importantly, Evan isn't beholden to special interests. He has one boss: The people of Indiana.

Friend, it's clear that Evan is the right choice for our state. **[Will you pitch in $5 today to make sure he gets to the Senate to fight for us?](https://bayh.bsd.net/page/m/32a504ca/7be36b49/1e987866/78f87e77/3197926088/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/bayh-email?refcode=20160925nd&amount=10")**

If Indiana elects Congressman Todd Young, we'll have a leader who thinks Social Security is a "fatally flawed" "Ponzi scheme." Who cares more about pleasing the Koch brothers than protecting Hoosier jobs. Who is, any way you slice it, much too extreme for Indiana. Hoosiers can't afford to have someone like that representing us in the Senate. But without your help, that's who we might end up with. "" Evan represents a path for Indiana and our country that is the direct opposite of Congressman Young's. He's repeatedly stood up for Indiana's working families by voting to protect Social Security and Medicare and eliminate discrimination by health insurance companies. As governor, he worked across the aisle to balance our state's budget and cut taxes. That 's the kind of Hoosier common sense we need in Washington! Most importantly, Evan isn't beholden to special interests. He has one boss: The people of Indiana. Friend, it's clear that Evan is the right choice for our state. "" Thank you, Emily Gurwitz Finance Director "" Paid for by the Evan Bayh Committee, 133 W. Market Street #153, Indianapolis, IN 46204 *
